_comment_overview: Two separate builds run in parallel:
1. React frontend (client/) — built as a static site via @vercel/static-build
2. Express backend (server/) — deployed as a serverless function via @vercel/node
Routing priority (evaluated top-to-bottom, first match wins):
/*     → React static build (SPA fallback: always serves index.html so
client-side React Router routes work on hard refresh)
All client API calls must use the /api prefix in production so they are
routed to the Express function rather than the SPA fallback.
client/.env.production sets REACT_APP_SERVER_URL=/api for this reason.
Express receives the full path including /api (e.g. GET /api/auth/me),
so server/index.js mounts a path-strip middleware that removes the /api
prefix before the request reaches the route handlers.

builds._comment: Build the React app from client/. @vercel/static-build runs `npm run build` and serves the output from the distDir.
Deploy server/index.js as a Node.js serverless function. @vercel/node wraps the exported Express app so Vercel can invoke it per-request.

routes._comment: Route all /api/* requests to the Express serverless function. The full path (including /api) is forwarded; server/index.js strips the prefix.
SPA fallback: everything else is served from the React build output. React Router handles the URL on the client side.
